memoryArtifacts.headline: Richard Ingersoll and Ann Langley by Myrtle S. Hyde

popularStory.headline: Life Sketch of Richard Ingersoll
popularStory.description:
Richard Ingersoll was christened in March 1587 in Edworth, Bedfordshire, England.  He was the son of George Ingersoll.

     Richard married Anne (Agnes) Langley in October 1611 in Sandy, Bedfords …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Ingersoll於1587年3月10日出生在英國貝德福爾艾德沃斯時，他的父親George Inkerstall24歲，母親Alicia Hankin28歲。他於1611年10月10日與Ann Langley在英國貝德福爾桑迪結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1629年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼普萊姆斯。他去世於1644年7月21日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Ingersoll于1587年3月10日出生在英国貝德福爾艾德沃斯时，他的父亲George Inkerstall24岁，母亲Alicia Hankin28岁。他于1611年10月10日与Ann Langley在英国貝德福爾桑迪结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1629年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼普萊姆斯。他去世于1644年7月21日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: Ingersoll
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Inkersall in Staveley (Derbyshire). The placename may derive from an Old English personal name Hynkere (genitive Hynkeres) ‘limper’ + Old English hyll ‘hill’. Compare Ingerson 2.

History: The Ingersoll name in America dates back to John Ingersoll, who emigrated to the Massachusetts Bay Colony in 1629. His descendants include lawyers, public officials, and politicians in CT and PA.

variants.name: Ingerson
meanings: English (Devon): from the Middle English personal name Inger + -son. Inger is from the Old Norse name Yngvarr, Ingvar, whose later form was Ívarr (see Iverson 2).

Americanized form of Swedish Ingersson and probably also of Danish Ingersen, respectively an extinct and very rare patronymics from the male personal name Inger, from Old Norse Ingvarr, a compound of Ing (the name of a Norse god associated with fertility) + arr ‘warrior’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Ingersoll was born on 10 March 1587, in Edworth, Bedfordshire, England, his father, George Inkerstall, was 24 and his mother, Alicia Hankin, was 28. He married Ann Langley on 10 October 1611, in Sandy, Bedfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He immigrated to Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1629. He died on 21 July 1644, in Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Ingersoll est né le 10 mars 1587, à Edworth, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, George Inkerstall, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Alicia Hankin, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Ann Langley le 10 octobre 1611, à Sandy, Bedfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Il a immigré au Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1629. Il est décédé le 21 juillet 1644, à Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 57 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Ingersoll è nato il il 10 marzo 1587 nel Edworth, Bedfordshire, England, il suo padre, George Inkerstall, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Alicia Hankin, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Ann Langley il 10 ottobre 1611, nel Sandy, Bedfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Immigrato nel Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America nel 1629. Morì il 21 luglio 1644, nel Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Ingersollが1587年3月10日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアエドウオルスで生まれた時，父George Inkerstallは24歳，母Alicia Hankinは28歳であった。1611年10月10日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアサンディでAnn Langleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1629年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニープリモウスに移住した。彼は1644年7月21日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスサレムで57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Ingersoll(이)가 1587년 3월 10일 영국 베드퍼드실르 에드워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Inkerstall(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Alicia Hankin(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1611년 10월 10일 영국 베드퍼드실르 샌디에서 Ann Langley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1629년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 플림우스로 이민을 떠났다. 그는 1644년 7월 21일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살렘에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Ingersoll nasceu em 10 de março de 1587, em Edworth, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, George Inkerstall, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Alicia Hankin, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Ann Langley em 10 de outubro de 1611, em Sandy, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele imigrou para o Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1629. Ele faleceu em 21 de julho de 1644, em Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Ingersoll родился 10 марта 1587 г. в местности эдворт, бедфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени George Inkerstall было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Alicia Hankin, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Ann Langley 10 октября 1611 г. в местности сэнди, бедфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он иммигрировал в плимоут, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1629 году. Он умер 21 июля 1644 г. в местности сеилем, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Ingersoll nació el 10 de marzo de 1587, en Edworth, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, George Inkerstall, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Alicia Hankin, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Ann Langley el 10 de octubre de 1611, en Sandy, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Inmigró a Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America en 1629. Murió el 21 de julio de 1644, en Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 57 años.
